questions.question: A student compares two p-block elements and claims that group position alone is enough to predict their common oxidation states. Which correction is most consistent with NCERT reasoning?
options: Group position gives a trend, but inert pair effect and element size can change the stability of oxidation states.
Group position is irrelevant because p-block oxidation states are decided only by atomic mass.
Oxidation states remain fixed across a group because valence shell configuration is identical.
Only electronegativity decides oxidation state stability, so group trends are not useful.

questions.question: Statement I: Down a p-block group, lower oxidation states may become more stable. Statement II: This can be linked to reluctance of s-electrons to participate in bonding. Which option evaluates the reasoning?
options: Both statements are correct, and Statement II explains the trend through inert pair effect.
Both statements are correct, but Statement II explains only metallic character, not oxidation states.
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect because s-electrons always participate equally.
Statement I is incorrect because higher oxidation states become more stable down every p-block group.
